body: ## Summary When adding a custom **non-physical NPC** subclass for a “mystery client” contact, the in‑game **Messages app UI (and some other phone apps)** becomes corrupted and renders an unexpected UI (looks like a cartel/region UI with sliders instead of the normal conversation list). Removing the custom NPC type from the mod assembly restores the correct UI, even if our custom PhoneApp remains enabled. ## Environment - Game: **Schedule I** (version: 0.4.1f13) - S1API: **S1API** (version: 2.7.3) - Mod: **Khundian.S1.EscalatingOrders** - Runtimes tested: - Mono (MelonLoader) The behavior below is consistent on Mono ## Goal We want a **messaging-only NPC contact**: - No world presence (`IsPhysical = false`). - No dealer/customer/schedule behavior. - Used only as a named SMS contact for our “unknown client” that sends Escalating Orders messages. ## Minimal NPC Implementation Current (commented-out) implementation in our mod: ```csharp using S1API.Entities; using UnityEngine; namespace Khundian.S1.EscalatingOrders.Integration { /// Minimal non-physical NPC used as the "unknown client" contact /// for Escalating Orders SMS conversations. public sealed class UnknownClientNpc : NPC { public static UnknownClientNpc? Instance { get; private set; } public override bool IsPhysical => false; protected override void ConfigurePrefab(NPCPrefabBuilder builder) { // Identity only; no schedule / customer / dealer builder.WithIdentity("eo_unknown_client", "Unknown", "Client") .WithIcon(null); } protected override void OnCreated() { base.OnCreated(); Instance = this; } } } ``` Notes: - We’re using the **new pattern** (`ConfigurePrefab` + `WithIdentity`) as recommended in S1API docs, not the obsolete `NPC(string id, ...)` constructor. - `IsPhysical` is `false`, so this NPC should be non‑physical and invisible. - We do not configure customer, dealer, schedule, or relationship behavior in `ConfigurePrefab`. ## Initial Messaging Usage (now disabled) The original intent was to use this NPC for SMS offers: ```csharp // Inside MessagingIntegration if (UnknownClientNpc.Instance != null) { var text = BuildOfferText(order); // "New order [Tier]: ..." var responses = BuildOfferResponses(order); // Accept / Abandon responses _currentResponseOrderId = order.Id; UnknownClientNpc.Instance.SendTextMessage( text, responses, responseDelay: 1f, network: true); } ``` However, for debugging the UI issue, we later **disabled all uses** of `UnknownClientNpc` and still saw the glitch as long as the NPC class existed. ## Observed Behavior ### When the NPC subclass exists - Phone **Messages app** (and some other phone apps) render a completely different UI: - Looks like a cartel/region/relationship screen (bars, sliders, region names) rather than the standard messages list. - This happens even when: - Our custom Escalating Orders `PhoneApp` is compiled out / disabled. - We do not call `UnknownClientNpc` or `SendTextMessage` anywhere at runtime. ### When the NPC subclass is commented out - As soon as we comment out the `UnknownClientNpc` class (so there is no custom `NPC` subclass in our assembly): - The Messages app returns to its normal UI (list of contacts/messages). - Other phone apps that were glitching also render correctly. - Re‑enabling our custom `EscalatingOrdersApp : PhoneApp` with **no NPC present** does **not** cause any corruption. In short: > The presence of a custom `NPC` subclass alone (even unused at runtime) appears sufficient to corrupt the phone UIs. ## Experiments Performed 1. **PhoneApp disabled, NPC present** - Wrapped `EscalatingOrdersApp : PhoneApp` in a compile-time `#if` so it doesn’t build into the assembly. - Left `UnknownClientNpc` compiled and present. - Result: Messages app still glitched → PhoneApp is not the culprit. 2. **PhoneApp disabled, NPC commented out** - PhoneApp still compiled out. - Commented out the entire `UnknownClientNpc` class. - Result: Messages app and other apps returned to normal. 3. **PhoneApp enabled, NPC still commented out** - Re-enabled `EscalatingOrdersApp : PhoneApp` (two-column layout similar to ModsApp using `UIFactory`). - `UnknownClientNpc` remained commented out (no custom NPC subclasses). - Result: Messages app and other apps still normal. Our phone app works, and other apps are unaffected. 4. **NPC implementation style** - Initially used the obsolete `NPC(string id, ...)` constructor and saw logs like: > `[NPC] [Relationship Data] FinalizeNetworkSpawn: No NPCPrefabIdentity component found for NPC 'eo_unknown_client', will try type-based defaults` - Switched to the new `ConfigurePrefab` pattern with `WithIdentity`, which removed this log, but the UI glitch persisted until the NPC class itself was removed from the assembly. ## Questions 1. Is there a **recommended pattern** for creating a messaging-only NPC contact (non-physical, no world presence) that: - Appears as a stable contact in the Messages app, but - Does **not** affect other phone apps or UI systems? 2. Are there any additional configuration steps we should take in `ConfigurePrefab` or elsewhere (e.g., conversation categories, relationship data, schedule setup) to keep such NPCs isolated from world/relationship UIs? Any guidance or examples of a “pure messaging contact” NPC would be very helpful. We’d like to reintroduce the SMS accept/abandon responses in a way that plays nicely with the rest of the game’s UI. Thank you!
title: [BUG]Non-Physical NPC Corrupts Phone UI

body:
### Summary

Huge Storage Locker names are removed on save reload. Melon 0.7.0 and 0.7.1. Only occurs when S1API.ll2Cpp.MelonLoader.dll is in Mods folder. Having S1APILoader.MelonLoader.dll in Plugins + S1API.ll2Cpp.MelonLoader.dll in Mods causes names to break; removing S1API.ll2Cpp.MelonLoader.dll from Mods but keeping S1APILoader.MelonLoader.dll in Plugins does not break names. Occurred with multiple other mods installed, but also occurred when all mods were removed, and also occurred with fresh install of Schedule 1 game.

### Game Version

0.4.3f3

### S1API Version

2.9.8

### MelonLoader Version

0.7.0

### Runtime Environment

IL2CPP (Regular)

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Download S1API Forked to S1 folder
2. Extract files, verify S1API only in Mods and Plugins; no other mods
3. In-game, use clipboard to rename storage locker, verify name exists
4. Sleep in-game to save, verify name still exists
5. Quit save
6. Load save, observer storage locker

### Expected vs Actual Behavior

Expected: Storage Closet name held between save loads and restarts
Actual: Closet names disappear on save load and restart

### MelonLoader Log

https://limewire.com/d/dDlzS#c5ChiQGknS

### What Have You Tried?

- When issue appeared, tried removing all mods, closet names stayed when S1API removed from Mods folder
- Reinstalled S1 completely, added S1API to Mods and Plugins

### Additional Context

_No response_

### Pre-submission Checklist

- [x] I have read the installation guide
- [x] I am using the latest version of S1API
- [x] I have verified this issue is related to S1API (not general modding support)
- [x] I am using MelonLoader 0.7.0 or have noted compatibility issues with 0.7.1+
- [x] I have included my MelonLoader log or relevant excerpts
title: [BUG] Huge Storage Closet name removed on load

body:
### Summary

PhoneApp.OnDestroyed()  is not implemented, so overriding it does nothing.


### Game Version

Other (specify in Additional Context)

### S1API Version

3.0.1

### MelonLoader Version

Other (specify in Additional Context)

### Runtime Environment

IL2CPP (Regular)

### Steps to Reproduce

I was trying to clean up some Actions in my app using PhoneApp.OnDestroyed() thinking it was called when returning to the main menu. The below code is never executed, at least not that I saw.

protected override void OnDestroyed()
{
	LogManager.LogMessage("Unsubscribing OnLocaleChanged from OnLocalUpdated", LogLevel.Warning);
	LocalizationManager.OnLocaleUpdated -= OnLocaleChanged;
	base.OnDestroyed();
}

### Expected vs Actual Behavior

**Expected**: PhoneApp.OnDestroyed() is called when returning to the main menu from the game. I don't know where it _should_ be called, but that's at least one point I would expect.
**Actual**: PhoneApp.OnDestroyed() is never called

### MelonLoader Log

_No response_

### What Have You Tried?

_No response_

### Additional Context

Schedule 1: 0.4.5f
MelonLoader: 0.7.2

### Pre-submission Checklist

- [x] I have read the installation guide
- [x] I am using the latest version of S1API
- [x] I have verified this issue is related to S1API (not general modding support)
- [x] I am using MelonLoader 0.7.0 or have noted compatibility issues with 0.7.1+
- [x] I have included my MelonLoader log or relevant excerpts

body:
## Bug Description
Stuck on "Waiting for host..." screen when sleeping

## To Reproduce
Steps to reproduce:
1. Start server with [server_config.toml.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/27082692/server_config.toml.txt)
2. Wait till night
3. Sleep
4. Stuck on "Waiting for host..." screen

## Expected Behavior
Sleep and skip to the next day when everyone slept

## Actual Behavior
Stuck on "Waiting for host..." screen

## Environment
- DedicatedServerMod Version: 6.0.0
- MelonLoader Version: 0.7.2
- Game Version: 0.4.5f2
- OS: Linux with wine
- Build Type: Il2cpp_Server
- Runtime Scope: idk

## Logs
```log
there aren't any relevant things in the log

body:
## Bug Description
Running the `settime` server command from the console crashes the dedicated server. This appears to be specific to console execution; the command should reject invalid runtime state or handle the authoritative time update without taking down the process.

Relevant code path: `Server/Commands/BuiltIn/Gameplay/SetTimeCommand.cs` currently resolves `NetworkSingleton<TimeManager>.Instance` and calls `SetTimeAndSync(targetTime)` after parsing the `HHMM` argument.

## To Reproduce
Steps to reproduce:
1. Start a dedicated server.
2. Open the server/TCP console.
3. Run `settime 1800` or another valid `HHMM` value from console.
4. Observe the server crash.

## Expected Behavior
The console command should set the authoritative server time, or fail gracefully with an error message if the time manager/runtime state cannot safely process the request.

## Actual Behavior
The server crashes when `settime` is run from console.

## Environment
- DedicatedServerMod Version: 0.9.1
- MelonLoader Version: 0.7.2
- Game Version: 0.4.5f1
- OS: Windows 10
- Build Type: Il2cpp_Server
- Runtime Scope: Il2Cpp

## Logs
N/A
title: [BUG] Console settime command crashes server

body:
## Bug Description
IL2CPP Docker server fails to start on Linux/Wine after the game has been installed successfully. SteamCMD completes the default-branch install, MelonLoader starts, but the process crashes during the .NET/CoreCLR startup phase with a Wine `virtual_setup_exception stack overflow`.

Mono runtime on the same host works and reaches `DEDICATED SERVER READY`.

## To Reproduce
Steps to reproduce:
1. Use the published Docker image / patched local image based on:
   `ghcr.io/ifbars/s1dedicatedservers:0.9.2-beta`
2. Set environment:
   - `S1DS_RUNTIME=il2cpp`
   - `FORCE_STEAMCMD_UPDATE=true`
   - `STEAM_BRANCH=` unset/empty, so runtime default branch is used
3. Start the container with Docker Compose on Linux.
4. SteamCMD installs Schedule I successfully from the default branch.
5. The container applies MelonLoader/bootstrap files and starts the game server with Wine.
6. Observe crash during MelonLoader/.NET/CoreCLR startup.

## Expected Behavior
The IL2CPP dedicated server should start under Docker/Wine and reach the dedicated server startup sequence, similar to the Mono runtime, eventually logging something like:

```log
=== DEDICATED SERVER ONLINE ===
=== DEDICATED SERVER READY (orchestrator) ===
Waiting for client connections...
````

## Actual Behavior

The IL2CPP server does not reach the dedicated server ready state. It crashes during Wine/MelonLoader/.NET startup with a stack overflow.

Relevant log excerpt:

```log
DedicatedServerMod runtime: il2cpp
DedicatedServerMod bootstrap DLL: DedicatedServerMod_Il2cpp_Server.dll
App ID: 3164500
Install directory: /home/steam/game
Steam branch: default
Success! App '3164500' fully installed.
Game installation/update completed successfully
Installing/updating Steamworks redistributables (app 1007, anonymous)...
Success! App '1007' fully installed.
Applying MelonLoader bootstrap files...
Writing steam_appid.txt (3164500)
Copying Steamworks native DLLs into game directory...
Steam DLL presence check:
  MISSING: steam_api64.dll
  OK: steamclient64.dll
  OK: tier0_s64.dll
  OK: vstdlib_s64.dll
  OK: steamclient.dll
  OK: tier0_s.dll
  OK: vstdlib_s.dll
  OK: steam_appid.txt
Starting game server with Wine...
Launch args: -batchmode -nographics --dedicated-server --require-auth --auth-provider steam_game_server --messaging-backend steam_networking_sockets --steam-gs-anonymous
WINEDLLOVERRIDES=version=n,b;steam_api64=n,b;steamclient64=n,b;tier0_s64=n,b;vstdlib_s64=n,b;steamclient=n,b;tier0_s=n,b;vstdlib_s=n,b;version=n,b

[MelonLoader.Bootstrap] Downloading the .NET Runtime installer...
[MelonLoader.Bootstrap] Installing the .NET Runtime...
0108:fixme:amsi:AmsiInitialize L"coreclr", ...
0108:fixme:amsi:AmsiScanBuffer ...
0108:err:virtual:virtual_setup_exception stack overflow 336 bytes addr 0x5073c2a8 stack 0x20eb0 (0x20000-0x21000-0x120000)
```

## Environment

* DedicatedServerMod Version: `0.9.2-beta`
* Docker Image: `ghcr.io/ifbars/s1dedicatedservers:0.9.2-beta`
* MelonLoader Version: `0.7.2 Open-Beta`
* Game Version: default branch IL2CPP install via SteamCMD
* OS: Linux Docker host
* Wine/Proton: `Wine/Proton 8.0.2`
* Build Type: `Il2cpp_Server`
* Runtime Scope: `IL2CPP only`

## Additional Notes

Mono runtime on the same Docker host works successfully after switching to:

```env
S1DS_RUNTIME=mono
STEAM_BRANCH=
```

Mono logs:

```log
Game Type: MonoBleedingEdge
Game Version: 0.4.5f2 Alternate
DedicatedServerMod runtime: mono
DedicatedServerMod bootstrap DLL: DedicatedServerMod_Mono_Server.dll
DedicatedServerHost v0.9.2-beta
=== DEDICATED SERVER ONLINE ===
FishNet server started successfully
=== DEDICATED SERVER READY (orchestrator) ===
Waiting for client connections...
```

This suggests the Docker setup, SteamCMD credentials, ports, and general Wine execution are working, while the IL2CPP runtime path fails during MelonLoader/.NET/CoreCLR startup.

There was also an earlier packaging issue with the published image where `/home/steam/run.sh` had CRLF line endings, causing:

```log
/home/steam/run.sh: line 2: $'\r': command not found
syntax error near unexpected token `$'{\r''
```

I worked around that by building a local image with:

```dockerfile
FROM ghcr.io/ifbars/s1dedicatedservers:0.9.2-beta

USER root
RUN sed -i 's/\r$//' /home/steam/run.sh && chmod +x /home/steam/run.sh

USER steam
ENTRYPOINT ["/home/steam/run.sh"]
```

After that patch, Mono works, but IL2CPP still fails with the stack overflow shown above.

```
title: [BUG] Ubuntu

curation.label: good
curation.reason: Real ScheduleOne issue with useful maintainer context for calibrating ghi issue quality scoring.

body:
## Bug Description
When starting the server the error

[ERROR] [DedicatedServerHost] Authentication backend initialization failed: Failed to initialize Steam game server API (gamePort=38465, queryPort=27016, mode=eServerModeAuthentication) and no logged-in Steam user API fallback was available

appears, even though I set my Steam-GS token

## To Reproduce
Steps to reproduce:
1. Follow the Guide for setup
2. Set Steam-GS token
3. Start server

## Expected Behavior
Server starts and connects to Steam authentication.

## Actual Behavior
Server starts but does not connect to Steam authentication.

## Environment
- DedicatedServerMod Version:  v0.9.3-beta
- MelonLoader Version:  0.7.3
- Game Version:  0.4.5f2
- OS: Windows 11
- Build Type: Il2cpp_Server
- Runtime Scope: IL2CPP only

## Logs
```log
[14:46:59.199] [WARNING] [Il2CppInterop] Class::Init signatures have been exhausted, using a substitute!
[14:46:59.225] [Il2CppInterop] Registered mono type MelonLoader.Support.MonoEnumeratorWrapper in il2cpp domain
[14:46:59.304] Support Module Loaded: C:\Users\Timo\Desktop\Schedule I\MelonLoader\Dependencies\SupportModules\Il2Cpp.dll
[14:47:01.380] [DedicatedServerHost] === Dedicated Server Starting ===
[14:47:01.416] [DedicatedServerHost] Steam game server token set and anonymous login disabled
[14:47:01.419] [DedicatedServerHost] Using default save location: C:\Users\Timo\Desktop\Schedule I\UserData\DedicatedServerSave
[14:47:01.420] [DedicatedServerHost] Tip: You can set a custom 'saveGamePath' in server_config.json to use a different save folder.
[14:47:01.445] [Il2CppInterop] Registered mono type Il2CppInterop.Runtime.DelegateSupport+Il2CppToMonoDelegateReference in il2cpp domain
[14:47:02.670] [ERROR] [DedicatedServerHost] Authentication backend initialization failed: Failed to initialize Steam game server API (gamePort=38465, queryPort=27016, mode=eServerModeAuthentication) and no logged-in Steam user API fallback was available
title: [BUG] Authentication backend initialization failed
